justification: All pass criteria met: both terminology mismatches (session/token and suspended/locked) explicitly identified and mapped in terminology_alignment section; brief uses consistent terminology throughout with clear notes on origin (Bird vs MJ); both ACs preserved with exact error codes SESSION_EXPIRED and ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED; architecture details include Redis TTL 1800 and last_accessed_at field. The suspended/locked mismatch is appropriately flagged as an open question requiring confirmation rather than silently resolved, which exceeds the rubric requirement.

observations.text: terminology_alignment section explicitly maps both pairs with clear domain_term/technical_term structure and definitions noting which agent used which term
Correctly flags suspended/locked as unresolved rather than assuming equivalence — open question directs Shaq to confirm with Bird and MJ before implementing
Both error codes SESSION_EXPIRED and ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED preserved exactly in acceptance criteria
Redis TTL 1800 and last_accessed_at field included in architecture guidance with correct sliding window semantics
Synthesis notes outside the brief provide additional context on why the mismatches matter, giving Shaq full traceability
Open questions section anticipates downstream implementation risks — e.g., whether ACCOUNT_LOCKED would need a separate error code if states are distinct

handoff_brief:
  recipient: Shaq
  task_context: >
    Build the user authentication feature for the auth service. This includes
    session/token lifecycle management with a 30-minute sliding expiration window,
    and account status enforcement that blocks suspended/locked accounts from
    establishing new sessions. The feature has two primary enforcement points:
    idle session expiry (returning 401) and account status checks at login (returning 403).

  domain_rules:
    - rule: A
